title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: Architecture has always been more than the mere construction of shelters; it is a discipline that (0) shapes the way we experience the world around us. Urban planners and architects are increasingly (1) attention to the psychological impact of building design on residents. Research suggests that high-rise structures can sometimes (2) feelings of isolation, whereas open, communal spaces tend to (3) social interaction. This is particularly relevant in densely populated cities, where the (4) of space often leads to vertical living. Furthermore, the use of natural materials, such as wood and stone, has been (5) to lower stress levels among office workers. Environmental psychologists refer to this as "biophilic design," a concept that (6) to integrate nature back into the urban environment. It is not just about aesthetics; the physical environment (7) a crucial role in determining our overall well-being. By (8) the human element into account, designers can create cities that are not only functional but also emotionally supportive. As urban populations continue to grow, understanding this link between the built environment and the human mind will become essential for building the resilient and inclusive communities of the future, ensuring that our cities remain both habitable and truly vibrant.

example.explanation: “Shapes” is the standard verb for influencing experiences or abstract concepts. “Crafts” usually refers to physical objects.

questions.explanation: “Pay attention to” is the standard fixed collocation. While “turning attention to” is possible, “paying” fits the increasing focus described here.
“Trigger feelings” is a common collocation in psychology. “Provoke” is usually used for anger or reactions, not internal states like isolation.
“Foster social interaction” is a standard academic collocation meaning to encourage or promote development.
“Shortage of space” is the correct collocation for a lack of available area in cities.
“Linked to” is the correct collocation for scientific or research-based correlations.
“Seeks to [verb]” is a formal construction used to describe the aim or intention of a concept or person.
“Plays a role” is a fixed idiomatic expression. The other verbs do not collocate with 'role' in this context.
“Take into account” is a fixed idiomatic phrase meaning to consider something.
